Discord Quick Start: A Hands-on Walkthrough of the Community App

I've been moving over to Discord lately — I used to live on Slack — so I figured I'd write a basic Discord usage walkthrough covering the home view, servers, channels, threads, and a few other essentials.
Why Does async/await Quietly Break Inside forEach? (And How to Fix It)

forEach is one of those methods we reach for constantly, but the moment you need to do something synchronous inside it — like firing AJAX requests in order — it falls apart. This post digs into why forEach ignores your await, and walks through a couple of ways to fix it.
npm ci vs npm install: What's the Difference?

Most of us are used to running `npm install` when restoring a project, but since npm 5.7.1 there's been a recommendation to use `npm ci` instead. This post walks through why, and when each one is the right call.
JavaScript Core Concepts (68) - ES6 Arrow Functions: Chapter Exercise

We've finally reached the end of the "ES6 Arrow Functions" chapter, so let's take a look at the exercise it has in store for us. Two short problems, both built around the same trap: arrow functions don't have their own `this`. If you can spot why each snippet breaks and rewrite it correctly, you've got this chapter down.
A Quick Trick for Resetting Vue Data to Initial Values
We often need to reset data to its initial values during development. This post is a quick tip for doing exactly that — and the trick works on both Vue 2 and Vue 3.
Nuxt.js 2.x Hands-On Handbook (3) — The Mysterious .nuxt Folder
We've built up a bunch of folders so far — dynamic routes, nested routes, and so on. How do we verify they actually work inside Nuxt.js? Time to crack open the mysterious `.nuxt` folder.
Last chapter we got the basic Nuxt.js example environment ready. Now let's walk through the overall Nuxt.js architecture.
How to Find Your Discord ID (Desktop + Mobile)

I was building a Discord Bot recently and noticed that finding your own Discord User ID is weirdly fiddly — and a lot of people get the wrong thing. So this post walks through how to grab it correctly, including the mobile flow.
Nuxt.js is a framework built on Vue.js, Node.js, and Webpack. I've got some Nuxt.js experience from past projects, so I figured I'd write a series to document it.
Arrow Functions in Practice: Where They Actually Shine (and Where to Stop)

Now that we've covered how arrow functions work, let's look at where you'll actually use them day to day. Mostly that means pairing them with array methods like map and reduce, where they really cut down the noise. We'll rewrite a couple of regular functions as arrow functions and see how much shorter they get — plus a quick warning about when not to over-shorten.
